text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. Salah (namaz/prayer) is the second pillar of Islam. It must be performed five times daily: Fajr (dawn), Dhuhr (noon), Asr (afternoon), Maghrib (sunset), and Isha (night). Before praying, one must perform wudu (ablution), face the Qiblah (direction of Mecca), and make the intention (niyyah) for the specific prayer.

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 1: Takbeer
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. Stand facing the Qiblah. Raise both hands until the thumbs are parallel to the earlobes. Say 'Allahu Akbar' (Allah is the Greatest). This is called Takbeer al-Tahreema (the opening takbeer). For women, raise hands to shoulder level.

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 2: Standing Position (Qiyam)
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. Place the right hand over the left hand beneath the navel (men) or on the chest (women). Recite Thanaa: 'Subhaanak Allaahumma wa bihamdika wa tabaarak asmuka wa ta'aala jadduka walaa ilaaha ghayruk' (Glory be to You, O Allah, and praise be to You. Blessed is Your name and exalted is Your majesty. There is no god but You).

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 3: Ta'awwuz and Tasmiyyah
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. Recite Ta'awwuz: 'A'udhu billahi min ash-shaytaan ir-rajeem' (I seek refuge in Allah from the accursed Satan). Then recite Tasmiyyah: 'Bismillah ir-Rahman ir-Raheem' (In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful).

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 4: Surah Al-Fatiha
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. Recite Surah Al-Fatiha: 'Al-hamdu lillahi rabbil 'aalameen. Ar-Rahman ir-Raheem. Maaliki yawmid-deen. Iyyaaka na'budu wa iyyaaka nasta'een. Ihdinas-siraatal mustaqeem. Siraatal-ladhina an'amta 'alayhim, ghayril-maghdoobi 'alayhim walad-daaalleen.' Say 'Ameen' at the end.

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 5: Additional Surah
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. After Al-Fatiha, recite any other surah or at least 3 verses from the Quran. Common choices include Surah Al-Ikhlas, Surah Al-Kawthar, or Surah Al-Falaq. This additional recitation is required in the first two rak'ahs of every prayer.

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 6: Ruku (Bowing)
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. Say 'Allahu Akbar' and bow down for ruku'. Place hands on knees with fingers spread wide. Keep the back straight, head level with back (not raised or lowered). Recite 'Subhaana Rabbiyal Azeem' (Glory be to my Lord, the Magnificent) at least three times.

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 7: Rising from Ruku (Qawmah)
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. Rise from ruku' while saying 'Sami'Allahu liman hamidah' (Allah hears those who praise Him). Stand up straight with hands at your sides. Then say 'Rabbanaa lakal hamd' (Our Lord, to You belongs all praise).

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 8: Sujud (Prostration)
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. Say 'Allahu Akbar' and go down for sujud. First place your hands on the ground, then your knees, then your nose and forehead between your palms. Seven parts should touch the ground: forehead with nose, both palms, both knees, and toes of both feet facing Qiblah. Recite 'Subhaana Rabbiyal A'la' (Glory be to my Lord, the Most High) at least three times. Men: keep arms away from body, stomach away from thighs. Women: keep body compact.

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 9: Sitting Between Sujud (Jalsah)
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. Say 'Allahu Akbar' and sit up. Sit on the left foot with the right foot erect (toes pointing towards Qiblah). Place hands on thighs. Pause briefly, then say 'Allahu Akbar' and perform the second sujud in the same manner as the first. This completes one rak'ah.

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 10: Second Rak'ah
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. After the second sujud, rise to standing position (say 'Allahu Akbar'). Do not recite Thanaa in subsequent rak'ahs - go directly to Ta'awwuz and Tasmiyyah, then Al-Fatiha and an additional surah. Perform ruku' and sujud as before. After the second sujud of the second rak'ah, remain seated for Tashahhud.

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 11: Tashahhud (Attahiyyat)
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. In the sitting position after two rak'ahs, recite Tashahhud: 'At-Tahiyyatu lillahi was-salawatu wat-tayyibatu. As-salamu 'alayka ayyuhan-Nabiyyu wa rahmatullahi wa barakatuh. As-salamu 'alayna wa 'ala 'ibadillahis-saliheen. Ashhadu alla ilaha illallah, wa ashhadu anna Muhammadan 'abduhu wa rasuluh' (All compliments, prayers and pure words are due to Allah. Peace be upon you, O Prophet, and the mercy of Allah and His blessings. Peace be upon us and upon the righteous servants of Allah. I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and I bear witness that Muhammad is His servant and messenger). Raise the index finger of the right hand when saying 'la ilaha illallah'.

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 12: Durood (Salawat)
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. After Tashahhud in the final sitting, recite Durood Ibrahim: 'Allahumma salli 'ala Muhammadin wa 'ala aali Muhammadin, kamaa sallayta 'ala Ibrahima wa 'ala aali Ibrahima, innaka Hamidun Majeed. Allahumma barik 'ala Muhammadin wa 'ala aali Muhammadin, kamaa barakta 'ala Ibrahima wa 'ala aali Ibrahima, innaka Hamidun Majeed' (O Allah, send prayers upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad, as You sent prayers upon Ibrahim and upon the family of Ibrahim. Indeed, You are Praiseworthy, Glorious. O Allah, bless Muhammad and the family of Muhammad, as You blessed Ibrahim and the family of Ibrahim. Indeed, You are Praiseworthy, Glorious).

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 13: Dua Before Salam
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. After Durood, recite a dua. A recommended dua: 'Allahumma inni zalamtu nafsi zulman kathiran, wala yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa anta, faghfir li maghfiratan min 'indika warhamni, innaka antal-Ghafoor ur-Raheem' (O Allah, I have wronged myself greatly, and none forgives sins except You. So grant me forgiveness from You and have mercy on me. Indeed, You are the Forgiving, the Merciful).

reference: How to Pray Salah - Step 14: Salam (Ending the Prayer)
text: How to pray. Prayer guide. Namaz steps. Turn your head to the right and say 'As-salamu 'alaykum wa rahmatullah' (Peace and mercy of Allah be upon you). Then turn your head to the left and repeat the same words. This concludes the prayer. After salam, it is sunnah to recite: 'Astaghfirullah' (3 times), then 'Allahumma antas-Salam wa minkas-salam, tabarakta ya dhal-jalali wal-ikram'.

reference: Number of Rak'ahs in Each Prayer
text: Fajr: 2 sunnah + 2 fard = 4 total. Dhuhr: 4 sunnah + 4 fard + 2 sunnah + 2 nafl = 12 total. Asr: 4 sunnah + 4 fard = 8 total. Maghrib: 3 fard + 2 sunnah + 2 nafl = 7 total. Isha: 4 sunnah + 4 fard + 2 sunnah + 2 nafl + 3 witr = 17 total. The fard (obligatory) rak'ahs are: Fajr (2), Dhuhr (4), Asr (4), Maghrib (3), Isha (4).

Women's prayer differs in these ways according to the Hanafi school: (1) Raise hands to shoulder level, not earlobes. (2) Place right palm on back of left hand on the chest, not below navel. (3) In ruku', bend only slightly (just enough to reach knees), keep fingers together. (4) Keep limbs close to body in ruku' and sujud (not spread out). (5) In sujud, rest forearms on the ground. (6) In sitting position, sit on the left buttock with both legs to the right side. The principle is that women adopt the most concealing posture in prayer.

Before praying, one must be in a state of wudu (ritual purity). Wudu involves: (1) Washing hands 3 times, (2) Rinsing mouth 3 times, (3) Sniffing water into nostrils 3 times, (4) Washing face 3 times, (5) Washing arms to elbows 3 times, (6) Wiping head with wet hands once, (7) Wiping ears once, (8) Washing feet to ankles 3 times. Wudu is broken by: using the toilet, passing gas, sleeping lying down, bleeding, or vomiting.
